user_story: As a collection manager, I want to assign specific permissions to users for viewing, editing, or deleting records, so that I can ensure proper access control and data security in my collection.
true_user_story: As a collection manager or cataloger I want to be able to see who last edited a record and when.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to assign specific edit permissions to users for each record, so that only authorized users can modify sensitive information.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has create/update/delete access to records in my collection.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to view an audit log of all changes to records, so that I can track who made edits and ensure accountability.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has edit access to individual records in my collection.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to assign specific edit permissions to users for individual records, so that only authorized staff can modify sensitive information.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict access to collection metadata.
As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to edit an arbitrary number of Items at once so that I can make changes to all Items in a collection efficiently.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to select multiple Items and update their metadata in bulk, so that I can efficiently manage large collections.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to use a grid/spreadsheet style interface so I can edit Items quickly.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be notified if required metadata is missing when publishing a record, so that I can ensure all necessary information is included before making it public.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to save a record only when the record has an identifier so that we don't end up with a bunch of empty records in the collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to save a record as long as it has an identifier, even if other metadata is missing, so that I can start records with minimal information and complete them later without creating empty entries.
